content_markdown:
A cell with negligible internal resistance is connected as shown. The ammeter and the voltmeter are both ideal.

What changes occur in the ammeter reading and in the voltmeter reading when the resistance of the variable resistor is increased?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a cell connected in series with an ammeter and two resistors. The first resistor is a variable resistor with a voltmeter connected in parallel across it. The second resistor is a fixed resistor.

vibe_explanation: Increasing the resistance of the variable resistor increases the total resistance of the circuit, which decreases the total current (ammeter reading). Since the variable resistor now takes up a larger proportion of the total resistance in the series combination, the potential difference across it (voltmeter reading) increases according to the potential divider principle.
